entries.name: ctx
entries.description: Attacker took over abandoned package and uploaded versions that exfiltrate AWS credentials from environment variables
indicators: sends AWS credentials to remote endpoint

entries.name: phpass
entries.description: Companion malicious PyPI package alongside ctx hijack; exfiltrates AWS credentials

entries.name: colorsama
entries.description: Typosquat of colorama; exfiltrates Discord tokens, browser cookies, system info

entries.name: djanga
entries.description: Typosquat of django; installs information stealer

entries.name: pip-helper
entries.description: Dependency confusion attack package masquerading as internal Python utility; collects host data and posts to attacker URL

entries.name: ultralytics
entries.description: GitHub Actions cache poisoning combined with a script-injection in the release workflow pushed two malicious versions of the popular YOLO/object-detection package to PyPI; users were dropped XMRig cryptominer on install.

entries.name: torchtriton
entries.description: Dependency-confusion attack against the PyTorch nightly channel: an attacker uploaded a malicious `torchtriton` to public PyPI, which `pip` resolved in preference to PyTorch's private index. The package exfiltrated ~/.ssh, /etc/hosts, and environment vars.

entries.name: aiocpa
entries.description: Backdoor added to a previously-clean async CryptoPay client; new release shipped an obfuscated payload that stole wallet keys. Detected by Phylum.

entries.name: request
entries.description: Long-running typosquat of `requests`; one of the most-installed accidental imports on PyPI. Various authors have used the name to push installer-stage payloads.

entries.name: python-requests
entries.description: Typosquat of `requests` using the obvious `python-` prefix; multiple malicious uploads under this name historically.

entries.name: requests3
entries.description: Originally a legitimate experimental fork, the name is repeatedly registered by attackers between abandoned periods. Treat as suspicious unless installed from the canonical author.

entries.name: colourama
entries.description: British-spelling typosquat of `colorama`; published a clipboard hijacker that replaced cryptocurrency addresses in copied text.

entries.name: python-colorama
entries.description: Persistent typosquat of `colorama` using the `python-` prefix; ESET tracked dozens of uploads dropping `cstealer`-style infostealers.
